name: Raspberry-Peach Pie
recipeIngredient: 2 cups peaches (fresh or frozen), thawed and drained, juices reserved
2 cups raspberries (fresh or frozen), thawed and drained, juices reserved
1/2 cup hazelnuts; pecans; or walnuts, chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375 degrees F.
2. Roll out dough for the bottom crust and place in a 9-inch pie pan. Trim and chill.
3. To make the filling, mix flour and sugar together in a bowl.
4. Add drained peaches, raspberries, nuts, and optional rum. Stir in half of the reserved juices until well mixed.
5. Spoon the mixture into the chilled crust.
6. Dot with butter.
7. For the top crust, brush the rim of the bottom crust with water to help it adhere.
8. Press on a lattice crust or place a full crust over the filling, piercing as needed.
9. Brush with glaze made from sugar dissolved in water.
10. Bake for 3/4 hour in the hot oven until the crust is golden and juices are bubbling.
Variation: Substitute blueberries for raspberries.
